title: Daily Brief and Digest for SaaS Companies
metaDescription: Get a daily morning brief for your SaaS business: pipeline, trials, churn signals, and support load, summarized before your first meeting.
excerpt: A done-for-you morning brief that pulls trials, pipeline, churn signals, and support load into one digest, delivered before your first meeting of the day.

sections.intro:
If you run a SaaS company, your morning probably starts with five tabs open. You check the CRM for new trials, the billing tool for failed charges, the support inbox for fires, and the analytics dashboard for whatever moved overnight. By the time you have a real picture of the business, half the morning is gone and you have not done any actual work yet.

The AIOS Daily Brief and Digest fixes that. It reads from the tools you already use, pulls the numbers and events that matter, and lands one short summary in your inbox before your first meeting. You see what changed, what needs a decision, and what can wait. No dashboards to open. No tabs to chase.
sections.fourCFit:
This capability sits in the Cadence layer of your AI Operating System. Cadence is the layer that runs on a schedule without being asked, so the work happens while you sleep or focus on something else.

  • Context: the brief knows your ICP, your pricing tiers, and which metrics you actually care about.
  • Connections: it reads from your CRM, billing system, support inbox, product analytics, and calendar.
  • Capabilities: it summarizes, ranks, and flags items that need a human decision.
  • Cadence: it runs every morning before you start, and again as a lighter end-of-day digest if you want one.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
You get one email or chat message at a set time each morning. It is short, scannable, and written in your voice.

  • Delivered by 7:30am local time, or whatever start time you pick.
  • New trials and signups from the last 24 hours, with source and plan.
  • Pipeline movement: new deals, stage changes, and deals gone quiet for more than 7 days.
  • Revenue events: new MRR, expansions, downgrades, and failed charges.
  • Churn signals: cancellations, support tickets from accounts above a revenue threshold, and usage drops on key accounts.
  • Support load: open tickets, oldest unanswered, and any flagged escalations.
  • Calendar preview: your next three meetings with one-line context for each.
  • One clear section called "Needs your decision today" with no more than five items.
sections.howWeInstall:
We install the Daily Brief in three phases. You are not configuring anything yourself.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at your stack, your metrics, and what your current morning routine actually involves. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we connect your CRM, billing, support, and analytics to the AIOS, write the brief template in your voice, set the delivery time, and run it in shadow mode for three days so you can correct the format before it goes live.
  3. Operate (ongoing): we tune the brief weekly based on what you actually read and act on, and we add one new automation per week to the wider system. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Reclaim 30 to 60 minutes every morning that used to go to tab-checking and dashboard-hopping.
  • Catch failed charges and churn signals 1 to 2 days earlier on average, which usually recovers 2 to 5 percent of at-risk MRR.
  • Faster decisions on the five items that actually matter, instead of skimming twenty that do not.
  • Standing guarantee: across your full AIOS, you recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Does this replace our analytics dashboard?

No. Dashboards are still useful when you want to dig in. The Daily Brief is the layer on top that tells you whether you need to dig in at all on a given morning.

What if our team uses different tools than the average SaaS company?

The AIOS is tool-agnostic. As long as your CRM, billing system, support inbox, and analytics have a way to share data, we connect to what you already run on. We do not ask you to switch tools.

Can the brief go to more than one person?

Yes. We often set up a founder version with everything and a lighter team version that only includes the sections relevant to that person, like a sales-only or support-only digest.
